entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Wearable fitness tracker used as a data source for workout observations

entities.name: Bio-Log System
entities.context: Workout logging and tracking system with SQLite backend that ingests data from multiple sources

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database used to store workouts and workout_observations tables

entities.name: Manual Text Input
entities.context: Alternative workout data source (source='manual_text') for logging workouts outside Fitbit

entities.name: Idempotent Re-import
entities.context: Design principle where duplicate Fitbit imports are safely ignored by checking external_id

entities.name: Canonical Metrics
entities.context: Authoritative workout measurements (duration, distance, calories, avg_hr) that can be updated when observations are linked

entities.name: Multi-source Observation Model
entities.context: Architecture where a single workout can have multiple observations from different sources (Fitbit, manual) linked via workout_id

relationships.context: Fitbit serves as a primary automated data source for workout observations
Manual text input is an alternative source for logging workouts when Fitbit data is unavailable
System stores workout and observation data in SQLite with workouts and workout_observations tables
Fitbit imports check external_id to prevent duplicate observation insertion
Workouts can have multiple observations; manual entries attempt to link to existing Fitbit workouts on the same date
When a manual observation links to a Fitbit workout, canonical metrics may be updated with richer data
